SpTU 1, 082 [Physiognomic omens varia][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] TUKU x [...]

[...] has ... [...]

o 2'2'

[...] RA [...]

[...] ... [...]

o 3'3'

[...] ⸢NAM⸣.LU₂.U₁₈.LU i-[...]

[...] humanity ... [...]

o 4'4'

[...] ⸢:⸣ * KI.MIN<(...)> SAG.DU SAG.<<BU>>.UŠ mar-[ra-aṣ]

[...]. : (If) ditto, the head is normal: he is sickly.

o 5'5'

[...] mu-šam-ni-[iḫ]

[...]: he is one who tires easily.

o 6'6'

[...]-pu-ut i-⸢šar⸣

[...] ...: he will be well.

o 7'7'

[...] : * KI.MIN<(...)> SAG.⸢DU⸣ ṣe-ḫi-ir i-šar

[...]. : (If) ditto, the head is small: he will be well.

o 8'8'

[... ba]-aʾ-il i-šar

[...] it is exceptionally large: he will be well.

o 9'9'

[...] ⸢mu⸣-šam-ni-iḫ : * KI.MIN<(...)> SAG.KI si-i-qu i-šar

[...]: he is one who tires easily. : (If) ditto, the forehead is narrow: he will be well.

o 10'10'

[... i]-šar : * KI.MIN<(...)> SAG.KI SIG₇ i-šar

[...]: he will be well. : (If) ditto, the forehead is yellow: he will be well.

o 11'11'

[...] ⸢BA⸣.UŠ₂ : * KI.MIN<(...)> SA SAG.KI-BI te-bi u SIG₇ mar-ra-aṣ

[...]: he will die. : (If) ditto, the vein on his forehead is pulsing and yellow: he is sickly.

o 12'12'

[...]-ru i-šar

[...] ...: he will be well.

o 13'13'

[...]-la mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] ...: he is one who tires easily.

o 14'14'

[...] x-muš mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] ...: he is one who tires easily.

o 15'15'

[...] x-ti i-šar

[...] ...: he will be well.

o 16'16'

[...] x-lu i-šar

[...] ...: he will be well.

o 17'17'

[... na]-ki-id mar-ra-aṣ

[...] is throbbing: he is sickly.

o 18'18'

[...] x [...] * KI.MIN<(...)> IGI GUB₃-šu₂ kap-ṣa-at TI.LA

[...] ... [...]. (If) ditto, his left eye is squinting: he will recover.

o 19'19'

[...] ZALAG₂ra TI.LA

[...] are bright: he will recover.

o 20'20'

[...]-⸢kur⸣-ra-na-a mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] are ...: he is one who tires easily.

o 21'21'

[...]-pa-sa mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] are ...: he is one who tires easily.

o 22'22'

[...]-x-qa i-šar

[...] are ...: he will be well.

o 23'23'

[...] pa-al-da : pa-lu-da BA.UŠ₂

[...] are paldu (or): palludu: he will die.

o 24'24'

[...] ⸢ur?⸣-ru-pa BA.UŠ₂

[...] are darkened: he will die.

o 25'25'

[...] maḫ-ḫa BA.UŠ₂

[...] are swollen: he will die.

o 26'26'

[...]-⸢ik⸣-ki-a BA.UŠ₂

[...] are ...: he will die.

o 27'27'

[...]-x-a : zi-ra-a TI.LA

[...] are twisted: he will recover.

o 28'28'

[...]-x-a TI.LA

[...] are ...: he will recover.

o 29'29'

[... ti]-⸢iq⸣-qa-a TI.LA

[...] are flashing: he will recover.

o 30'30'

[...]-ra BA.UŠ₂

[...] are ...: he will die.

o 31'31'

[... ba]-u₂-la BA.UŠ₂

[...] are very large: he will die.

o 32'32'

[...]-da BA.UŠ₂

[...] are ...: he will die.

o 33'33'

[... na]-⸢wi⸣-ir mar-ra-aṣ

[...] is bright: he is sickly.

o 34'34'

[...] ⸢ṣu₂⸣-ur-ru-up i-šar

[...] is dark red: he will be well.

o 35'35'

[... da]-aʾ-im i-šar

[...] is dark: he will be well.

o 36'36'

[...] SIG₇ BA.UŠ₂

[...] is yellow: he will die.

o 37'37'

[... na-am]-ru mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] are bright: he is one who tires easily.

o 38'38'

[... da-aʾ]-mu i-šar

[...] are dark: he will be well.

o 39'39'

[... pe-ṣu₂]-u₂ ul-tab-bar

[...] are white: he will live long.

o 40'40'

[... pe-lu]-u₂ ul-tab-bar

[...] are light red: he will live long.

o 41'41'

[...] mar-ra-aṣ

[...]: he is sickly.

o 42'42'

[...] ⸢nam⸣-ru mu-šam-ni-iḫ

[...] are bright: he is one who tires easily.

(rest of obverse missing)
